L1.area: Server-side request handling and data persistence

L2.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists where user-supplied path components are converted to local filesystem paths using a naive character replacement approach, allowing crafted paths to escape the intended data directory and access arbitrary files on the system.

L3.area: Filesystem-based storage backends for calendar and contact data
L3.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists where user-supplied path components are converted to local filesystem paths using a naive character replacement approach. On Windows, a crafted path containing a drive letter prefix can escape the intended data directory and allow reading or writing arbitrary files on the system.

ground_truth.reason: The filesystem storage backend converts URL paths to local filesystem paths by naively replacing forward slashes with the OS path separator, allowing crafted paths like '/c:/file/ignore' to escape the data directory on Windows.
